items.title: Scanner self-matches the marker pattern in source
items.description: P0 defect in the backbone of the autonomous loop: a scanner that matches its own source will inject phantom backlog items every cycle, corrupting every downstream sprint. This must be resolved before any other scanner/parser work is trusted. It also unblocks items ranked 8 and 9.

items.title: broken parser — export const z = 3
items.description: Flagged P0 and sourced from a docs/plans file rather than production TypeScript; the title contains a code-snippet artifact, strongly suggesting the scanner itself (see rank-1 bug) injected this. Assigned a low confidence score accordingly. Should be investigated and closed or re-triaged once the self-match bug is fixed; do not block the sprint on it.

items.title: CycleResult needs an `error` field for FAILED stage
items.description: A missing `error` field on CycleResult means all FAILED-stage outcomes are opaque to the CycleRunner and any human approver. Adding it is low-risk, small-surface, and unblocks meaningful error telemetry for every subsequent cycle. No dependencies.

items.title: Test suite pollutes .agentforge/ at runtime
items.description: Test-suite filesystem pollution causes non-deterministic failures and taints the real runtime state directory, making it hard to distinguish CI failures from production anomalies. Fixing this improves signal quality for every future autonomous cycle and prevents cost-wasting retry storms.

items.title: Branch name concatenation produces double "v" when…
items.description: Double-"v" branch names (e.g. `v-v6.4`) cause git pushes and PR creation to target unexpected branch names, silently breaking the autonomous PR loop. A small string-normalisation fix prevents an entire class of cycle failures downstream of cycle-runner.

items.title: PROpener must not request review from the PR author. GitHub…
items.description: GitHub rejects review requests where the reviewer is the PR author, causing pr-opener API calls to fail hard. This is an active regression risk every time an agent opens a PR for its own work. Fix is well-scoped: filter author from reviewers list before the API call.

items.title: PROpener should warn-and-skip on unknown labels instead…
items.description: Hard-failing on unknown labels turns a benign labelling mismatch into a full cycle abort. Switching to warn-and-skip makes the PR opener resilient to label drift between repos and reduces unnecessary human escalation.

items.title: Strip comment-delimiter artifacts ("-->") from parsed lines
items.description: Comment-syntax leakage into backlog titles ("-->", "*", "//") pollutes item titles displayed in the dashboard and in PR descriptions. Cleanup is straightforward regex work in proposal-to-backlog; best done after the self-match bug (rank 1) is resolved to avoid masking re-injected noise.

items.title: X --> don't include "-->" in extracted title
items.description: Companion cleanup to rank-8: specifically strips trailing arrow-comment markers from extracted titles. Small, focused fix; naturally batched with rank-8 work in the same file. Depends on rank-1 being resolved first for the same reasons.

items.title: add a one-sentence description of the autonomous loop to this file
items.description: Low-cost documentation hygiene that improves onboarding clarity. Sourced from a plan doc rather than live source; confidence is moderate. Schedulable any time without risk of regression.

items.title: add a comment to README.md explaining the autonomous loop marker
items.description: README clarity improvement that aids future contributors in understanding the TODO(autonomous) convention. Minimal effort, zero regression risk, batches well with other doc tasks at end of sprint.

items.title: add a meaningful comment to this file — export const x = 1
items.description: The title contains an inline code snippet strongly suggesting this is a scanner false-positive artifact (code example inside a plan document, not a real source file). Should be verified before acting; if confirmed a false positive, close without action and use as a regression test case for rank-1's fix.

items.title: migrate workspace-adapter to postgres — export const x = 1
items.description: Tagged breaking and sourced from a plans document rather than production source, with an embedded code snippet in the title — high probability of being a scanner false-positive. Even if genuine, a postgres migration carries substantial regression risk across the entire persistence layer and should not run autonomously within a $50 cycle without human confirmation. Deprioritised to last; estimated cost reflects the real effort if actioned.
